title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA expects nearly 1 million attendees for South Boston's St. Patrick's Day parade on March 15.
Red Line will operate on a 'rush hour' schedule with potential Broadway Station skips due to crowding.
MBTA offers shuttle buses from South Station, with buses and commuter rail lines running modified schedules.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record 81.3% in 2025, trailing the state's 89.3% average.
Boston's dropout rate fell to 3.6% in 2025, marking the lowest rate on record outside of 2021.
Graduation rates improved across all demographics, with significant gains for English learners and students with disabilities.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins struggle with an 11-14-5 record on the road this season, impacting their playoff chances.
Boston Bruins sit in the second wild-card spot in the East with a 35-22-6 record, totaling 76 points.
Of the Bruins' remaining 21 games, 12 are away, including key games against playoff contenders and the Blue Jackets.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehab's pediatric services.
Wishart, Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine at Spaulding Rehab, aims to enhance pediatric care through fundraising efforts.
Spaulding's mission is to integrate cutting-edge research into clinical care, ensuring all children have access to top treatments.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children in Massachusetts have died from the flu this season, according to the Department of Public Health.
A total of 298 adults have also succumbed to influenza in Massachusetts as of February 28, state data shows.
38.1% of Massachusetts residents received the flu vaccine this season, while flu activity was reported low in late February.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The NBA canceled the Atlanta Hawks' Magic City promotion scheduled for March 16 after receiving significant backlash.
Luke Kornet's open letter criticized the promotion for involving a strip club, sparking widespread discussion and concern.
The canceled promotion included plans to feature Magic City's chicken wings and a performance by hip-hop artist T.I.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unfamiliar with Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman during a recent Inside the NBA episode.
Baylor Scheierman, a 2024 first-round pick, averages 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds per game for the Celtics.
Scheierman has maintained consistent playing time, even with Jayson Tatum back from injury on a minutes restriction.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cut ties with ICE over deportation flights at Hanscom Field.
Signature Aviation is the sole fixed base operator serving ICE at Hanscom, facilitating deportation flights under Trump's campaign.
Healey criticizes ICE for violating constitutional rights and urges Signature to reconsider its role in supporting ICE operations.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet into a shaft after a metal grate collapsed outside Boston's Omni Parker House.
Boston Inspectional Services issued a building code violation to the hotel, citing rotted supporting brackets.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect all vent grates and submit a repair report.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to serenade customers, posting signs for musicians at the station.
A retired violist has agreed to perform when warmer weather arrives, but Kertzman still welcomes more musicians.
Kertzman's unique idea was inspired by old films featuring strolling musicians, aiming to enhance the gas station experience.
